title: A hand-rolled factorial via structural recursion equals Nat.factorial
informal_statement: Define myFactorial via explicit structural recursion on Nat.rec (independent of Mathlib's Nat.factorial); for every natural number n, myFactorial n equals Nat.factorial n.
informal_proof_idea: myFactorial is defined as fun n => Nat.rec 1 (fun k ih => (k + 1) * ih) n -- a from-scratch structural recursion mirroring Nat.factorial's own defining equations, submitted as a helper `def` alongside the root theorem via the proof-search environment's SubmitModule action (rather than an existing Mathlib definition). The equality with Nat.factorial is then proved by ordinary induction: the zero case is rfl (both sides reduce to 1), and the successor case uses that myFactorial (n+1) reduces definitionally (via Nat.rec's iota rule) to (n+1) * myFactorial n, matched against Nat.factorial_succ and closed with the inductive hypothesis.
formal_statement_pp: theorem myFactorial_eq_factorial (n : ℕ) : myFactorial n = Nat.factorial n

review.review_basis: repo self-review; kernel-verified through the tracked proof-search loop

notes: Proof authority: Lean kernel via the tracked proof-search loop (episode 99b8de59), kernel_verified on the first attempt via a SubmitModule action (one helper `def myFactorial` + the root theorem), not a single Solve tactic block. This is the induction domain's first packet produced via SubmitModule -- every prior packet used Solve only, so recursion / helper-definition authoring was previously undemonstrated in this domain. The packaged Lean file states `n` as an explicit theorem parameter (dropping the tracked episode's leading `intro n`), matching this repo's established convention that a theorem header's explicit binders are already in scope before the tactic block starts, identical to the post-`intro` state.
